
```{r setup-03, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(arrow)
library(tidytext)
library(ggwordcloud)
library(stopwords)
library(scales)

df <- read_parquet("../data/processed/articles_with_sentiment.parquet") |>
  mutate(published_date = as.Date(published_date))

# Language code → stopwords source language
lang_map <- c(
  en = "en", it = "it", es = "es", fr = "fr",
  de = "de", pt = "pt"
)

# Build a combined stopwords list for all languages
all_stopwords <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(lang_map), function(lang) {
    tryCatch(
      tibble(
        word     = stopwords(language = lang, source = "snowball"),
        language = lang
      ),
      error = function(e) tibble(word = character(0), language = lang)
    )
  })
)

# Tokenize: one row per word per article
tokens <- df |>
  select(article_id, country, language, full_text) |>
  unnest_tokens(word, full_text) |>
  # Remove pure numeric tokens
  filter(!str_detect(word, "^\\d+$")) |>
  # Remove stopwords per language
  left_join(all_stopwords, by = c("word", "language")) |>
  # Keep only rows where stopword match is NA (i.e., NOT a stopword)
  # Trick: anti_join style via left join + filter
  # We mark stopwords, then remove them
  mutate(is_stop = !is.na(language)) |>
  # Actually redo this properly:
  # (tidytext anti_join approach doesn't handle multilingual well, so we do it manually)
  select(-is_stop)

# Proper multilingual stopword removal
tokens_clean <- tokens |>
  anti_join(all_stopwords, by = c("word", "language")) |>
  filter(nchar(word) > 2)
```

## Word Clouds per Country

```{r wordclouds, fig.height=12, fig.width=14, warning=FALSE}
top_words <- tokens_clean |>
  count(country, word, sort = TRUE) |>
  group_by(country) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 80) |>
  ungroup()

top_words |>
  ggplot(aes(label = word, size = n, color = n)) +
  geom_text_wordcloud(rm_outside = TRUE, seed = 42) +
  scale_size_area(max_size = 10) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
  facet_wrap(~country, ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13)) +
  labs(title = "Most frequent words per country (native language, stopwords removed)")
```

## TF-IDF: Most Distinctive Words per Country

TF-IDF measures which words are *distinctive* to each country relative to the full corpus.

```{r tfidf, fig.height=11, fig.width=12}
tfidf <- tokens_clean |>
  count(country, word, sort = TRUE) |>
  bind_tf_idf(word, country, n) |>
  group_by(country) |>
  slice_max(tf_idf, n = 10) |>
  ungroup()

tfidf |>
  mutate(word = reorder_within(word, tf_idf, country)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = tf_idf, y = word, fill = country)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~country, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  scale_y_reordered() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "Most distinctive words per country (TF-IDF)",
    subtitle = "Words with high TF-IDF are used much more by this country than others",
    x = "TF-IDF score", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

## Bigram Analysis

```{r bigrams, fig.height=11, fig.width=12}
bigrams <- df |>
  select(article_id, country, language, full_text) |>
  unnest_tokens(bigram, full_text, token = "ngrams", n = 2) |>
  separate(bigram, into = c("word1", "word2"), sep = " ") |>
  # Remove bigrams containing stopwords
  anti_join(all_stopwords, by = c("word1" = "word", "language")) |>
  anti_join(all_stopwords, by = c("word2" = "word", "language")) |>
  filter(nchar(word1) > 2, nchar(word2) > 2) |>
  filter(!str_detect(word1, "^\\d+$"), !str_detect(word2, "^\\d+$")) |>
  unite(bigram, word1, word2, sep = " ") |>
  count(country, bigram, sort = TRUE) |>
  group_by(country) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 10) |>
  ungroup()

bigrams |>
  mutate(bigram = reorder_within(bigram, n, country)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = bigram, fill = country)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~country, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  scale_y_reordered() +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  labs(
    title = "Most common two-word phrases per country",
    x = "Count", y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))
```
